title: ficus benghalensis var. krishnae
intro: Ficus benghalensis var. krishnae, also known as Banyan Fig, is a variant of the Ficus benghalensis tree, characterized by its aerial roots and broad, leathery leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin disorders, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral compounds. The health benefits of Ficus benghalensis var. krishnae include reducing oxidative stress, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as decoctions and infusions, are used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
